items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices Highlights: Benchmark equity indices Sensex and Nifty tumbled nearly 1% on Wednesday, snapping a three-day gaining streak due to heavy selling in IT stocks and an uptick in crude oil prices.

items.title: Rupee nears 94/USD level; settles 39 paise lower

items.title: DPIIT rolls out multi-sectoral regulatory relaxations to bolster industrial supply chains
items.summary: The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade is ensuring continuous supply of fuel, gas, and essential raw materials. Measures include customs duty waivers and expedited licensing. Applications for CNG and CBG stations are processed quickly. Temporary storage relaxations for kerosene and a ban on ammonium nitrate exports are in place.

items.title: Trump extends Iran ceasefire — but market focus has already drifted elsewhere
items.summary: Prospects for further negotiations remain uncertain, with Tehran and Washington offering conflicting signals on the ceasefire's status.

items.title: McDonald's boss on abuse claims: 'I don't want to talk about the past'
items.summary: A BBC investigation in 2023 heard from more than 100 McDonald's workers in the UK claiming they faced sexual assault, harassment, racism, and bullying

items.title: Govt overhauls six-decade-old sugarcane law; seeks comments on draft by May 20
items.summary: The Centre is proposing a new Sugarcane (Control) Order 2026, replacing the 1966 version to integrate ethanol production, digital compliance, and factory approvals. This framework maintains core elements like FRR and payment deadlines while introducing a conversion formula for ethanol and a formal approval process for new factories.

items.title: NCLAT reserves order in Jaiprakash Associates insolvency case
items.summary: On 6 April, a Supreme Court bench, led by Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justice Joymalya Bagchi, directed the NCLAT to hear the matter urgently

items.title: The K-pop cash flow: South Korean beats power India's FDI remix
items.summary: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung's visit to New Delhi signals India's strategic push for large-scale FDI in advanced manufacturing and technology. Discussions focused on deepening capital flows, particularly in semiconductors, shipbuilding, and energy, aiming to position India as a preferred investment base amidst global supply chain shifts.

items.title: UK inflation jumps to 3.3% in March as Iran war sends fuel prices to highest rise since Ukraine invasion
items.summary: UK inflation rose to 3.3% in March due to surging energy costs from the Iran war. Motor fuel prices jumped 8.7%, while services inflation unexpectedly increased to 4.5%. This inflation outlook may impact interest rates, with economists now expecting a prolonged pause in rate hikes.

items.title: UK inflation rises after Iran war pushes up fuel prices
items.summary: The figures provide the first official look at the impact of the Iran war on the cost of living in the UK.

items.title: India likely to breach budgeted fiscal deficit target for FY27: BMI
items.summary: India's fiscal deficit may exceed its target, reaching 4.5 percent of GDP. This is due to government policies responding to the West Asia conflict. Measures include supporting key industries and firms. The government might restrict exports of crucial inputs like helium and sulphur. Energy and fertilizer subsidies are expected to rise. Public infrastructure projects could be deferred to manage expenditure.

items.title: India likely to grow 7% in FY27 on domestic demand, investments; inflation to remain in range: EAC-PM chairman Dev
items.summary: The economy’s resilience in the face of headwinds generated by the West Asia war could be demonstrated by the narrowing of India’s trade deficit to $20.67 billion in March, said S. Mahendra Dev, chairman of the Economic Advisory Council to PM.

items.title: World's biggest condom maker set to raise prices due to Iran war
items.summary: Malaysia-based Karex produces more than five billion condoms a year and supplies global brands like Durex and Trojan.

items.title: HCLTech stock plunges on weak guidance, AI-led growth concerns
items.summary: Missed targets, a softer outlook and early AI-led pricing pressure sent HCL Tech shares lower on Wednesday, echoing post-earnings declines at TCS and Wipro.

items.title: Akasa taps Hajj travel to offset other West Asia route cuts
items.summary: Hajj 2026 travel to Mecca in Saudi Arabia begins in April, with the first flights carrying pilgrims having landed in Jeddah on 18 April. This marks the start of the arrival window, which will continue until May 21, ahead of the main rituals set for May 25-29.

items.title: UK inflation rises to 3.3% as Iran war impact begins to hit
items.summary: UK inflation rose to 3.3% in March from 3.0% in February, driven by higher fuel prices amid Middle East tensions. The increase, slightly above expectations, has prompted forecasts of further inflation pressure, though the Bank of England is still expected to hold interest rates steady in the near term.

items.title: UK inflation jumps to 3.3% in March as fuel prices surge amid Iran war
items.summary: Fuel prices saw their largest increase for over three years, the ONS' chief economist said.

items.title: Short-term fluctuations, including FDI outflows closely monitored: RBI's Sanjay Malhotra
items.summary: RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra addressed financial leaders in New York, assuring them that short-term economic fluctuations are being closely watched. He detailed ongoing reforms aimed at boosting foreign investment and market integration. The Reserve Bank highlighted India's robust economic fundamentals, including low inflation and strong forex reserves, positioning the nation as a rapidly growing global economy.

items.title: M2P Fintech brings in CFO Vivek Seshadri as IPO groundwork begins
items.summary: The company was last valued at over $750 million in 2024 after raising nearly $100 million from Helios. In total, it has secured more than $200 million from investors including Tiger Global, Better Capital and MUFG Innovation Partners, the venture arm of Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group.

items.summary: Iran War: The Indian government is seen to focus on securing essential supplies, curbing business costs through subsidies and tax relief, and expanding credit support for small businesses, accordig to Fitch unit BMI. These steps aim to stabilize industries and protect jobs amidst global uncertainty.

items.summary: Global oil prices eased after the US extended its ceasefire with Iran, but a continuing naval blockade and the prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz kept supply concerns—and volatility—firmly in play.

items.summary: With India contributing the bulk of its volumes, the intracity delivery platform is shifting towards enterprise clients and tighter unit economics as competition moves beyond speed.

items.summary: Axis Bank Trustee Services chief executive Rahul Choudhary said the role of the trustee should entail ensuring the right clauses are included in the agreement and that the asset is closely monitored throughout.

items.title: Amazon signs $30 million deal to buy carbon credits from Indian rice farmers
items.summary: Amazon has signed a $30 million (around ₹280 crore) agreement with the Good Rice Alliance, backed by Bayer, Temasek’s GenZero and Shell, to purchase carbon credits generated by Indian rice farmers, marking one of the largest agricultural carbon credit deals globally and the first of its scale in India.

items.title: India seafood exports to US fall amid tariffs
items.summary: India’s seafood exports to the US fell 19.8% in volume and 14.5% in value in FY26, hit by reciprocal tariffs, the commerce ministry said. However, overall exports hit a record $8.28 billion. Growth in China, the EU and Southeast Asia helped offset the US decline.

items.summary: The firm, co-founded by Sam Altman, said it is "not responsible" for the attack at Florida State University

items.title: India, Taiwan get time till October to settle ICT tariff dispute
items.summary: India and Taiwan have requested the WTO's dispute settlement body to postpone a ruling on India's import duties on ICT products until October 27. Both nations are actively engaged in mutual discussions to resolve the dispute. This marks the ninth such delay, as previous requests were also granted.

items.title: From Epstein to sock puppets: Key takeaways from Kevin Warsh's Fed confirmation hearing
items.summary: He denied making a deal with Donald Trump on interest rates and backed "policy regime change" at the central bank.

items.title: Apple’s Cook Says He’s ‘Healthy’; Ternus Promises AI Products
items.summary: Apple Inc.’s Tim Cook, who’s stepping down as chief executive officer later this year, told employees on Tuesday that he is “healthy” and plans to serve as executive chairman for a long time.

items.title: Andhra Pradesh mango pulp industry seeks central support
items.summary: Mango processors in Andhra Pradesh face export challenges. A significant quantity of mango pulp is stuck due to shipping lane disruptions. Processors are requesting government support. They seek equity capital and credit guarantees. Moratoriums on loan payments are also requested. Inclusion in export facilitation schemes is a priority.

items.title: Tweak sought in capital norms for urban co-operative bank licence
items.summary: In an effort to invigorate the co-operative banking landscape, the Reserve Bank of India is contemplating a significant reduction in the minimum capital requirement for urban co-operative bank licenses. The suggested cap is set to decrease from ₹300 crore to a more accessible ₹200 crore.

items.title: WTO accepts request of India, Taipei to defer ICT import duty dispute ruling until Oct 2026
items.summary: India and Taiwan have secured a delay in a WTO ruling on India's import duties for certain IT products. The decision is now postponed until October 2026. Both nations are actively engaged in discussions to find a mutual resolution to this trade dispute. This marks the ninth such deferral requested by the parties involved.

items.title: HCLTech sees muted FY27 after fastest growth in three years
items.summary: India's third-largest IT services company clocked 5.95% year-on-year growth in consolidated revenue to $14.66 billion, beating a Bloomberg estimate of $14.1 billion by 45 analysts. However, net profit fell 4.05% y-o-y to $1.96 billion, compared to a rise of 7.6% in FY25.
